data.content_ref: inline:The target defines the strict-advantage regime for base error in (0.40, 0.70), but the problem context's hard set (where SC was imperfect) operates at a candidate base error of 0.01. The proposed mechanism relies on stochastic dominance of critic score distributions for wrong-majority vs right-majority sets, but at base errors near 0.01, wrong-majority sets are vanishingly rare (binomial probability of ≥3 errors out of 5 at e=0.01 is <1e-5). The target fails to bridge the gap between its claimed operating regime (0.40-0.70) and the repository's evidence base, and provides no experiment design showing how to artificially elevate base error to 0.40-0.70 within the 300k-token budget on this provider.
